mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the 5-day forward return signal falls below 0.02 during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >65% of months following inflation readings above the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of observations over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real gross domestic product signals expanding economic activity that lifts corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters following positive GDP growth readings.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity that forces earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances across a 10-year backtest.
